code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test14-NOV-2019Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine color/appearance, supporting renal dysfunction and possible tubular damage. HbA1c is low, suggesting no chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying causes of renal impairment and proteinuria.
